summary: Matched three player bonus functions in src/melee/pl/plbonuslib: pl_8003E4A4, pl_80040688, and pl_800407C8. The PR implemented pl_8003E4A4 using an inline item-kind normalization helper, corrected an argument passed to unk_cond in pl_80040688, adjusted integer-to-float conversion shape in pl_800407C8, and updated the pl_8003E4A4 header prototype slot type from u8 to int.

key_files.role: Implemented and matched pl_8003E4A4, moved match_item_kind before its new use, and made small matching fixes in pl_80040688 and pl_800407C8.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pl_8003E4A4 implementation and match_item_kind relocation
raw_diff: pl_800407C8 casts xCEC/xCF0 through u32
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists 3 new matches

key_files.role: Updated pl_8003E4A4 declaration to match the recovered function signature by changing slot from u8 to int.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: /* 03E4A4 */ void pl_8003E4A4(int slot, bool, void*, int)

what_changed.change: Replaced the placeholder comment for pl_8003E4A4 with a C implementation using Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2, a local seen[0x27] array, three natural for-loops, stale move table x7AC counters, and pl_80038788(slot, 0x9D, 1) when a configured threshold is reached.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added void pl_8003E4A4(int slot, bool arg1, void* arg2, int count)

what_changed.change: Moved inline int match_item_kind(int kind) earlier in plbonuslib.c so pl_8003E4A4 can use it; the helper maps ordinary item kinds 0..0x22 directly and maps 0xCD, 0xE1, 0xE2, and 0x28 to indices 0x23..0x26, otherwise -1.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: match_item_kind added before pl_8003E4A4 and removed from later location

what_changed.change: Changed pl_80040688 to call unk_cond(arg1, temp_r0) instead of unk_cond(arg1, arg2).
Changed pl_800407C8 average calculation setup so xCEC and xCF0 are cast through u32 before being assigned to float temporaries used by pl_CalculateAverage.
Changed the pl_8003E4A4 header prototype slot parameter from u8 to int.

smart_moves.move: Kept item-kind normalization in a small inline helper rather than duplicating the switch mapping inside pl_8003E4A4.
smart_moves.why_it_matters: The helper isolates the special item-kind to compact index mapping used for the 0x27-entry seen/counter arrays.

smart_moves.move: Updated the owning header when the recovered body used int slot for pl_8003E4A4.
smart_moves.why_it_matters: This keeps the declaration aligned with the matched function body and the PR explicitly verified with require-protos.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: plbonuslib.h slot type changed from u8 to int

smart_moves.move: Used normal loops for fixed-size initialization, input scan, and stale table update in pl_8003E4A4.
smart_moves.why_it_matters: The loops express repeated 0x27-entry array/table operations directly and align with the natural-loops standard.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 0x27; i++) loops in pl_8003E4A4

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovering a function body changes a parameter type, update the owning header in the same PR and verify prototype consistency.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pl_8003E4A4 definition uses int slot
raw_diff: plbonuslib.h declaration changed from u8 slot to int slot

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small normalization helpers can make matched code more reviewable when a function needs to map sparse game IDs into dense table indices.
For table-sized bookkeeping, natural fixed-bound loops can be the matched source form rather than unrolled generated-looking blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pl_8003E4A4 initializes seen[0x27] with a for-loop
raw_diff: pl_8003E4A4 scans count entries and updates 0x27 stale counters with for-loops

naming_conventions.convention: New helper name match_item_kind describes the local mapping role without renaming address-style global functions whose meanings remain only partially recovered.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: address-style functions pl_8003E4A4, pl_80040688, pl_800407C8 remain address-style

naming_conventions.convention: Local names in pl_8003E4A4 use concise role names such as slot, table, moves, seen, count, and i where the roles are evident from the implementation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Relocated an inline helper before the newly matched function that calls it, preserving helper reuse while satisfying C declaration/order requirements.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: match_item_kind added before pl_8003E4A4 and removed from later position

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Changed a call operand in pl_80040688 from arg2 to temp_r0 to reach the matched call shape.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report shows pl_80040688 changed from 86.53% to 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used explicit u32 casts before float temporaries in pl_800407C8 to match the average calculation code shape.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report shows pl_800407C8 changed from 81.90% to 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Verified the claimed matches with the project configure/build flow and received bot-reported matched-code improvements.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists 3 new matches and +1104 matched bytes

follow_up_queries: pl_8003E4A4 stale move table x7AC seen array
pl_800407C8 u32 cast xCEC xCF0 pl_CalculateAverage
plbonuslib prototype slot u8 int require-protos

changed_files: exact changed files and line counts
notes: No review comments were present in the loaded slice, so review_feedback is empty.
The PR body claims local configure/build verification; the loaded evidence does not include raw build logs.
The bot report supports 100% matches for the three listed functions, but this intake does not independently rerun builds or objdiff.
The functional reason for arg1 in pl_8003E4A4 is not evident from the diff because the parameter is unused in the recovered body.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototype updates should accompany recovered bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2563 matched pl_8003E4A4 by defining it with int slot and updating src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.h from u8 slot to int slot; the PR verification command included python configure.py --require-protos && ninja.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pl_8003E4A4 definition and header prototype change

accepted_candidate_records.title: Integer casts can be required for float-average code shape
accepted_candidate_records.text: In pl_800407C8, casting xCEC and xCF0 through u32 before assigning to float temporaries was part of the diff that bot-reported the function moving from 81.90% to 100.00%. Treat this as a narrow tactic tied to this function shape, not a global style rule.

source_update_candidates.title: plbonuslib contains player bonus stale-move table matching work
source_update_candidates.text: src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c and src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.h were the only files changed in PR #2563, which matched pl_8003E4A4, pl_80040688, and pl_800407C8. The diff shows stale move table fields x7AC, xCBC, xCC0, xCEC, and xCF0 in this path.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: stale move table field accesses
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scoping fact for future searches, but should be curated against current source/path facts before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize the pl_800407C8 u32-to-float casts into a broad style rule; the evidence only supports a narrow matching tactic for this function.
Do not infer reviewer intent or review-requested changes; no review comments were loaded.
Do not claim arg1's semantic role in pl_8003E4A4; the recovered body leaves it unused in the diff.
